name: Google Nest Hub
short_description: A countertop smart display with a touchscreen and the Google Assistant for voice and visual help around the home.
long_description: Originally launched in 2018 as the Google Home Hub and rebranded Nest Hub in 2019, it is a fixed 7-inch touchscreen display driven by the Google Assistant, with a later Nest Hub Max adding a camera. It anchors a household's smart-home controls, routines, timers, media, and ambient information, and is a canonical example of physical AI that lives as an always-present domestic appliance rather than a robot, making conversational assistance a piece of household infrastructure.

images.caption: A Google Home Hub (now Google Nest Hub) smart display on a table.
images.alt_text: A small smart display with a touchscreen sitting on a table, with a fabric-covered speaker base, showing the time and ambient information on screen.

sources.title: Google Nest Hub
sources.access_notes: Official Google Store product page for the 2nd-gen Nest Hub.

sources.title: Google Nest (smart speakers)
sources.access_notes: Release history: Google Home Hub launched Oct 22, 2018; rebranded Nest Hub May 2019; Nest Hub Max Sept 2019.

interactions.title: Sleep that the bedside table watches
interactions.description: Set the 2nd-gen Hub on a nightstand and its Soli radar reads your breathing and movement through the night, with the mic catching coughs and snores and ambient sensors logging room light and temperature, to hand you a sleep summary at breakfast.

interactions.title: Wake to a fake dawn
interactions.description: The screen slowly glows from navy to warm gold over the 5–30 minutes before your alarm, with optional birdsong rising underneath, so the room brightens before the buzzer ever sounds.

interactions.title: An idle slab that becomes a window of faces
interactions.description: When nobody is talking to it, the Hub falls back to a Photo Frame, cycling Google Photos Live Albums that auto-update with a particular kid or pet, or showing curated art and a clock instead.

interactions.title: Raise a hand across the kitchen to pause
interactions.description: On the camera-equipped Hub Max, Face Match recognizes who walks up and surfaces just their reminders and calendar, and a raised palm from across the room pauses the music — all processed on-device without sending video to Google.

interactions.title: Shout up the stairs without shouting
interactions.description: Say “Hey Google, broadcast: dinner's ready” and your voice plays out of every Nest speaker and display in the house at once, with whoever's downstairs able to reply straight back.

interactions.title: The kitchen gadget that quietly stopped cooking
interactions.description: People who bought the Hub for hands-free, step-by-step recipes found Google removed the cookbook and saved-recipe feature in 2024, leaving cooks to fall back on a crash-prone YouTube workaround and venting on the Nest forums.

interactions.title: A room that lifts its own brightness
interactions.description: Because the Hub already carries an ambient light sensor for Ambient EQ, a routine could let it nudge connected smart bulbs up or down to match the room as dusk falls — using sensing it does today only to tune its own screen.

interactions.title: Presence-aware quiet hours
interactions.description: The bedside Soli radar already knows when someone is in front of it; that same motion sensing could hold notifications and dim the display the moment it detects you've settled into bed, without a camera or a wearable.
